appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Gemini API Patterns' section to voidpilot-knowledge.md documenting: api_version v1beta requirement for all genai.Client instances, SessionResumptionConfig always-on behavior, and hardcoded fallback API key tech debt pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that GeminiLive always injects get_weather tool and handler into every instance. If a future feature needs true tool isolation (only its declared tools, no defaults), GeminiLive needs an opt-out mechanism. Workers should know about this implicit tool merging.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: brainstorm-tools-and-handlers review found GeminiLive.__init__ merges get_weather into all instances, meaning the brainstorm session will have 4 tools at runtime instead of the intended 3. The brainstorm module correctly passes only 3 tools, but GeminiLive overrides this.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that GeminiLive._handle_tool_call hardcodes scheduling='WHEN_IDLE' for all FunctionResponses, ignoring per-tool scheduling values returned by handlers. The brainstorm save handler returns SILENT scheduling, but this is overridden at runtime. A future fix should make GeminiLive read scheduling from handler return values.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: brainstorm-tools-and-handlers review found GeminiLive line 393 hardcodes WHEN_IDLE. This affects BE-TH-04 (save handler should use SILENT scheduling) at runtime, though the handler code itself is correct.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update fullstack-worker skill's test guidance to include: (1) Testing GeminiLive.start_session internals requires careful mock setup with call_count-based receive mocks to prevent infinite loops. (2) Relax TDD red-green-refactor requirement to allow implementation and tests in close succession when the implementation is straightforward.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: session-resumption worker noted mock setup complexity for GeminiLive testing. flash-worker-service worker wrote implementation and tests together rather than strict TDD.
